laws.title: Punishment for Murder
laws.meaning: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life and shall also be liable to fine.
laws.punishment: Death or life imprisonment with fine

laws.title: Punishment for Culpable Homicide not amounting to Murder
laws.meaning: Covers cases where death is caused with the intention of causing death or bodily injury likely to cause death, but the case does not amount to murder.
laws.punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years with fine

laws.title: Attempt to Murder
laws.meaning: Whoever does any act with the intention or knowledge that if by that act he caused death he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 10 years or life imprisonment if hurt is caused

laws.title: Grievous Hurt
laws.meaning: Eight kinds of grievous hurt are defined including emasculation, permanent loss of sight, permanent disfigurement, fractures, etc.

laws.title: Punishment for Rape
laws.meaning: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 10 years which may extend to life.
laws.punishment: Minimum 10 years rigorous imprisonment, may extend to life

laws.title: Punishment for Theft
laws.meaning: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment up to 3 years or with fine or both.
laws.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment with fine

laws.title: Punishment for Robbery
laws.meaning: Robbery involves theft accompanied by force or threat of force.
laws.punishment: Up to 10 years rigorous imprisonment with fine

laws.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
laws.meaning: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property shall be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment with fine

laws.title: Punishment for Kidnapping
laws.meaning: Whoever kidnaps any person shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine.
laws.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment with fine

laws.act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Definition of Domestic Violence
laws.meaning: Acts of physical, sexual, verbal, emotional or economic abuse against a woman in a domestic relationship constitute domestic violence.
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 20,000 or both

questions.text: What type of criminal incident occurred?
Is the accused known to the complainant?
Is the victim receiving medical treatment?
Is CCTV footage available from the incident location?

recommended_actions: Register FIR immediately at the nearest police station
Obtain medical examination and preserve MLC certificate
Secure CCTV footage before it is overwritten
Collect and preserve physical evidence at crime scene
Apply for victim compensation under NALSA scheme if applicable
Approach magistrate for Section 156(3) CrPC complaint if police refuse FIR
